attacking_style: Rarely attacks directly; prefers tactical breakaway positioning as highest GC rider to gain time without energy expenditure. Shows limited explosive climbing power on steep gradients when rivals accelerate.
team_tactics: Benefits from strong domestique support (Hindley, Fabro) on climbs and dual-leader strategies. Teams prioritize defending his position through tempo control rather than aggressive racing. Occasionally sacrificed for team sprint objectives despite GC position.
terrain_preference: Time trials are surprisingly strong relative to climbing focus (multiple sub-1-minute gaps, 16sec to Yates). Struggles on long 24km+ climbs and steep gradients. Performs better on rolling/wavy stages with positioning over pure watts.
vulnerability: Crumbles on extended mountain stages (dropped on 24km climb, abandoned before first categorized climb at Vuelta). Mechanical/crash prone at critical moments. Poor descending technique (55sec lost to apparel issues). Weak final kilometer positioning costs seconds repeatedly.
key_patterns: Uses breakaways as GC tool rather than attacking from peloton (2022 Vuelta top-6 jump confirmed twice)
Strong time trial form for climber repeatedly verified: sub-60sec gaps, won after 5-year drought, 2nd Tirreno
Loses time on descents and technical sections rather than pure climbing (apparel struggle, positioning errors)
Physically fragile on consecutive mountain stages: abandoned mid-Vuelta, dropped on long climbs multiple times
Benefits from dual-leader team strategies but sometimes sacrificed for stage wins despite GC position
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 crash eliminated domestique role; 2022 saw physical collapse and abandonment at Vuelta despite earlier breakaway success showing declining durability
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 12
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
